title: ladyginia afghanica
intro: Ladyginia afghanica, commonly known as the Afghan lavender, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues due to its calming and sedative effects. The therapeutic actions of Ladyginia afghanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve mood, and enhance cognitive function. Its bioactive constituents, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and β-pinene, are responsible for its medicinal properties and are known to possess antimicrobial, antispasmodic, and anti-anxiety effects.
intro_study: According to "Heliyon", ladyginia afghanica benefits may include relief from vaginal discharge, mastitis, and other women's health issues, as it is one of the 217 plant species used in Pakistan's rural communities to treat various gynecological complications.
